name: dsd-platformsh

metadata.name: dsd-platformsh
metadata.summary: A plugin for django-simple-deploy, supporting deployments to Platform.sh.

documentation:
# dsd-platformsh

A plugin for deploying Django projects to Platform.sh, using django-simple-deploy.

For full documentation, see the documentation for [django-simple-deploy](https://django-simple-deploy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

modules.name: dsd_platformsh.utils
documentation: Utilities specific to Platform.sh deployments.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.utils.get_project_name
documentation:
Get the project name from the output of `platform project:info`.

Command is run with `--format csv` flag.

Returns:
    str: project name

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.utils.get_org_names
documentation:
Get org names from output of `platform organization:list --yes --format csv`.

Sample input:
    Name,Label,Owner email
    <org-name>,<org-label>,<org-owner@example.com>
    <org-name-2>,<org-label-2>,<org-owner-2@example.com>

Returns:
    list: [str]
    None: If user has no organizations.

modules.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy
documentation: Manages all platform.sh-specific aspects of the deployment process.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy.simple_deploy_automate_all_supported
documentation: Specify whether --automate-all is supported on the specified platform.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy.simple_deploy_get_automate_all_msg
documentation: Get platform-specific confirmation message for --automate-all flag.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy.simple_deploy_get_platform_name
documentation:
Return the name of the platform that's being deployed to.

DEV: Consider returning a config object if there ends up being more info to share
back to core.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy.simple_deploy_deploy
documentation: Carry out platform-specific deployment steps.

modules.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy_messages
documentation: A collection of messages used in deploy_platformsh.py.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy_messages.confirm_use_org
documentation: Confirm use of this org to create a new project.
--- Dynamic strings ---
These need to be generated in functions, to display information that's
determined as the script runs.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy_messages.unknown_create_error
documentation:
Process a non-specific error when running `platform create`
while using automate_all. This is most likely an issue with the user
not having permission to create a new project, for example because they
are on a trial plan and have already created too many projects.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy_messages.success_msg
documentation: Success message, for configuration-only run.

functions.name: dsd_platformsh.deploy_messages.success_msg_automate_all
documentation: Success message, when using --automate-all.

modules.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer
documentation: Manages all platform.sh-specific aspects of the deployment process.

classes.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer.deploy
documentation: Coordinate the overall configuration and deployment.
--- Public methods ---

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._validate_platform
documentation:
Make sure the local environment and project supports deployment to
Platform.sh.

Make sure CLI is installed, and user is authenticated. Make sure necessary
resources have been created and identified, and that we have the user's
permission to use those resources.

Returns:
    None

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If we find any reason deployment won't work.
--- Helper methods for deploy() ---

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._prep_automate_all
documentation:
Intial work for automating entire process.

Returns:
    None: If creation of new project was successful.

Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If create command fails.

Note: create command outputs project id to stdout if known, all other
  output goes to stderr.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._modify_settings
documentation:
Add platformsh-specific settings.

This settings block is currently the same for all users. The ALLOWED_HOSTS
setting should be customized.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_platform_app_yaml
documentation: Add a .platform.app.yaml file.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_requirements
documentation: Add requirements for Platform.sh.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_platform_dir
documentation: Add a .platform directory, if it doesn't already exist.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._add_services_yaml
documentation: Add the .platform/services.yaml file.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._conclude_automate_all
documentation:
Finish automating the push to Platform.sh.

- Commit all changes.
- Call `platform push`.
- Open project.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._show_success_message
documentation: After a successful run, show a message about what to do next.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._check_plsh_settings
documentation: Check to see if a Platform.sh settings block already exists.
--- Helper methods for methods called from simple_deploy.py ---

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._validate_cli
documentation: Make sure the Platform.sh CLI is installed, and user is authenticated.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._get_platformsh_project_name
documentation:
Get the deployed project name.

If using automate_all, we'll set this. Otherwise, we're looking for the name
that was given in the `platform create` command.
- Try to get this from `project:info`.
- If can't get project name:
  - Exit with warning, and inform user of --deployed-project-name
    flag to override this error.

Retuns:
    str: The deployed project name.
Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError: If deployed project name can't be found.

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._get_org_name
documentation:
Get the organization name associated with the user's Platform.sh account.

This is needed for creating a project using automate_all.
Confirm that it's okay to use this org.

Returns:
    str: org name
    None: if not using automate-all
Raises:
    SimpleDeployCommandError:
    - if org name found, but not confirmed.
    - if org name not found

methods.name: dsd_platformsh.platform_deployer.PlatformDeployer._confirm_use_org
documentation:
Confirm that it's okay to use the org that was found.

Returns:
    True: if confirmed
    SimpleDeployCommandError: if not confirmed

Perform the initial deployment to Platform.sh.

If --automate-all is used, carry out an actual deployment.
If not, do all configuration work so the user only has to commit changes, and call
`platform push`.
